title: Gateron China Joy Switch Mechanical Keyboard Custom 45gf 50gf Pom Series 5 Pin Purple Blue CJ Translucent Mechanical Keyboard Switch (10 PCS, Box CJ Dark Blue 3Pin)
features: Package Included：Only CJ switches，you have four styles to choose from
Gateron CJ (China Joy) is the linear switches.Lifetime 80 million times.Stem and base is POM material.Box Switch Spring is 22mm gold plated.Normal Switch Spring is 15 mm gold plated.
We got lubed and unlubed,3 pin and 5 pin edition which can be picked for different customers

The Actuation force of all box Switches is 45 gf, and the Actuation force of all nomal 5pin Switches is 50 gf，The Actuation distance of all box Switches is 1.2mm, and the Actuation distance of all nomal 5pin Switches is 2mm
Ordinary distinguish whether it is lubricated by color, Box distinguish whether lubricated by 3pin and 5 products

reviews.title: ULTRA Smooth, ZERO scratch and SAVES your RGBs, what else do you want?
reviews.text: These are so smooth you wont even believe it until you put it up to your ear and will hear nothing going on on the inside of the switch. After trying the Oil Kings and being super impressed, I said fine lets try the CJ Dark Blue Lubed switch and see what the fuss is about and I am glad I did. It is the end game switch. Stop the search right here. These are dead smooth a 10 out of 10, OK are about 7-10 and the Gateron Red Pro are somewhere like 5-10. They are all hard to tell and distinguish between the smoothness but once you test them out to your ear/finger you will be able to tell. I don't believe you need to lube these because well, they come lubed and since they are so smooth I don't see a need. Also the benefit with this switch is that this CJ Dark Blue comes with a slot for the LED to shine through just fine, so you will save your RGB as I have and I am glad.

reviews.title: for apex pro

reviews.title: Great keys for $25
reviews.text: I really like these keys because it is very nicely built. The plastic is thick and there is some texture. The only problem with the texture part is that the A,W,S,D buttons are going to wear down and it will smooth out. I would appreciate if they can do something to make it more durable. I’d still recommend this for putty caps.

reviews.title: Great keycaps
reviews.text: i got these for my Apex Pro they look amazing on it about the only thing about them is more about the apex then the keycaps,the space bar only has one led on the keyboard in the center so does not light up the spacebar as much as the rest of the keys and is pretty noticeable...the kit came with everything needed to be used on several different keyboards that i have so i might get another set for my corsair boards next.

reviews.title: They look so good!
reviews.text: Made my Steelseries Apex 7 keyboard pop even more. They feel great with my Red Linear Switches. They are a little more expensive than some other brands, but I feel like it was better to go with the same brand since I have seen other people have issues with switching out the keycaps on Steelseries keyboards.

reviews.title: Love these!
reviews.text: Amazing PBT pudding keycaps, I absolutely love these! I chose these to match my SteelSeries keyboard and I'm very happy with them.

title: GAMDIAS Hermes E2 7 Color Backlit Gaming Mechanical Keyboard, 87 Keys, Blue Switches, Anti-ghosting, Multimedia Control Keys, with Metal Plate (Hermes E2)
description: Hermes E2 7 color spectrum mechanical gaming keyboard exceeds basic user demand and enriches the distinct design of the GAMDIAS Hermes Series. The Hermes E2 7 color spectrum is small but elegant; simple yet powerful and offers enhanced durability. By using small tenkeyless keyboard can save the space,and also makes the keyboard and mouse to become closer, so you can maximize the comfort and accuracy of your operation. GAMDIAS Certified Blue Switches with a 50 million actuation lifecycle, 7 color spectrum backlighting , WASD-Arrow key swap, Multimedia control keys, Keyboard lock, Windows key disable, and stylish/elegant metal plate design.

reviews.title: Quite decent for the price - better quality than I expected
reviews.text:
I was skeptical about purchasing this combo because of the cheap price and the sketchy reviews, but I'll admit I'm impressed. I only spent $22.50 for the entire combo (used/refurbished). I expected the mouse to be dirt cheap from what the reviews were saying, but it feels quite solid to me. I played some Boarderlands and some League of Legends on the combo last night and it was comfortable and they both held up well. The mouse has a dpi changer that works great. For me personally I think it may have too many modes (somewhere around 4 or 5). I personally just like having 2 or 3 because when you're in game it gets complicated to remember which dpi setting you are on and cycle through to the correct one all within a few seconds.

They keyboard isn't overly bright. It is kind of is hard to read the keys at an angle, but other than that, it seems to be a great keyboard so far.

This is a good buy, for the low price of $20-30, for a basic gamin setup.

Typing is also decently comfortable, just takes some getting used to since I am used to flat keys.

reviews.title: Poor quality - lasted about 3 months.
reviews.text: died after 3 months.  very disappointing because this was quite expensive.  I've had another gaming keyboard for over 3 years and it is still working.  Buy the Razor instead.  Backlit keyboard was nice while it lasted...

reviews.title: Five Stars
reviews.text: Works great and honestly the best keyboard I've used and I love the adjustable weights on the mouse!

reviews.title: Great key functionality
reviews.text:
I love, love, love this keyboard. The feel of the keys as they are pressed is great. I'm an old school typer (keyboarder) and I feel like these give me the feeling I was missing. I think I even type faster with this keyboard. I am not a gamer so I don't use it for those thing.
I bought this based on a review that was done on Craft Computing on YouTube.

reviews.title: Amazing gaming & multitasking keyboard – improved my gaming and my everyday typing.
reviews.text:
I purchased this keyboard to go with my first custom-built gaming rig that I built around 2 months ago. I had been getting so frustrated with my old standard Logitech keyboard and I wanted an upgrade. I am not new to gaming, but this was my first ‘serious’ build, so I went with the advice of many of my more experienced friends and purchased a mechanical keyboard.

I needed a keyboard that could multitask as well. I work as a web developer, and I am in grad school for marketing – I do a lot of typing. So I wanted something that was going to last and perform in all of these uses – and still improve my gaming experience. After doing a TINY bit of keyboard research, I chose the blue switches since I do a lot more “regular typing” than gaming.

I’ve had the keyboard for about 2 days, and in that time I’ve used it to play the DOOM Multiplayer Beta, and several hours of Fallout 4. So far this morning I’ve written 2 blog posts, one paper, and now this review :D.

My first impression of this product was it was HEAVY. Not in a bad way, just in a very sturdy was that was a sharp contrast to what I had had before in my standard keyboard. Since this is a desktop keyboard, there’s no need for this keyboard to be light. The other thing I noticed was the high quality braided USB cable. I must’ve missed that feature when I ordered because I was pleasantly surprised – the USB connector is also gold plated. I did not expect that for such a low price point of around $100. (Note: I did receive a discount on the product in exchange for my honest review.)

I also received this keyboard exceptionally fast. I ordered on a Thursday afternoon and got it Saturday morning – no Saturday delivery fee!

**Important** So the package comes with a guide that is very concise – pay attention! I got confused when I didn’t get a ‘new hardware found’ notification like the manual said. However, the rest of the instructions worked fine, so it must not have mattered. I’m on Windows 10.

I was surprised to find that the shortcut keys (like for volume and track control) worked without any software at all. To access the different light settings, you just press FN and select the pattern from the keyboard itself. I settled on FN+0 – It’s a responsive setting that lights up the keys you type and then lets them fade – very cool since I don’t want to have the keyboard lit all the time. I’ll admit though – this thing is pretty, when I leave my desk I’ve been quickly pressing FN+Ins to get it to do the wave effect if I’m gonna be away for a while.

So that’s just the extra stuff – let’s talk about the performance. I can without a doubt say this keyboard has made a difference in my playing. The game mode is essential – make sure you enable that when you launch a game. The DOOM multiplayer beta was probably a bad gauge since that game is ridiculously hard anyway – but I noticed a marked difference in my performance on Fallout 4. I’m not going to pretend to be a keyboard expert, all I can say is that the response time was way better.

And I am SO glad I went with blue switches. Writing papers, I feel like an old world typist – the mechanical and audible feedback I get from typing is so satisfying. I have noticed reduced typos from ‘accidental’ slips. The audible click sound doesn’t really sound like the bulky old keyboards, it’s more of a quick, clean clicking. It isn’t loud, but it’s NOT whisper quiet, which I prefer.

Pros:
- The rubber feet keep it in place on my desk (I am one of those crazy people who don’t use a slide-out keyboard tray).
- The keyboard is study, and well made. A great value for the money.
- No special software needed to change effects and settings, everything is controlled on the keyboard, and the instructions are only 1 page.
- The game features are useful! Locking the windows key, consecutive attack mode.
- Custom patterns – this is essential when gaming. I used it to highlight the keys used when playing.
- Excellent physical and audible feedback when typing – extremely satisfying.
- The confidence that comes with the 50 million actuation lifecycle (I type a LOT)

Cons:
- There weren’t any instructions on what to do if you never got the ‘new hardware found’ message when plugging in the keyboard. This confused me ' but I figured it out. If this happens to you – just proceed with the manual, as far as I can tell, it makes no difference.
- As far as I can tell, you can’t make ALL the keys light up one color. I might be overlooking something though. This might be a con for some.

I would say this keyboard is perfect for someone like me who wants a serious gaming keyboard, but is ultimately a multitasker. You will definitely notice a difference if switching from a non-mechanical keyboard.
The quality of this keyboard has me anxious to try other GAMDIAS products, and I highly recommend it to anyone looking for an upgrade!

title: 4-Port HDMI KVM Switch – 4K 60Hz – QHD 144Hz - Audio Output and USB Sharing – Mechanical & Gaming Keyboards Supported – HKS0401A2U
description:
This 4x1 HDMI KVM Switch allows you to share one set of “keyboard, video monitor, and mouse” (“KVM”) with up to four PC’s or video devices (Xbox, PS4, Firestick, etc). The switcher does not require software and works with Windows, MacOS, Unix/Linux, Raspberri Pi, etc. HDMI 2.0 protocol supports 4K@60hz and blazing 144hz@1440P, making this device a perfect fit for work-from-home professionals and high-end video gaming.
Key Features
- Supports DisplayPort, DVI, & VGA with approved adapters.  - Keyboard passthrough mode allows for better compatibility plus multimedia key functions  - Hotkey triggers assignable to either Scroll Lock or Right-CTRL  - USB sharing works with printers, jump drives, USB headsets and webcams for teleconferencing.  - Auto-scanning with adjustable time interval from 1 to 250 seconds.  - 1-year US-based warranty when purchased from BuyTESmart
FAQ
- Audio is transmitted via HDMI connection. HDMI or DisplayPort monitor required for audio  - Use powered USB hub to expand the USB2.0 port if multiple connections are needed  - Use USB extender to reduce distance to receiver if wireless mouse/keyboard is “jumpy”  - Please install CR2025 battery before use (battery not included)
Mouse / Keyboard Compatibility
Dedicated mouse & keyboard ports have emulation programming. Second gen chipsets have “Passthrough Mode” to send special characters such as multimedia keys (play, pause, volume up/down), which improves keyboard compatibility. Keyboard software such as Logitech Options, Corsair iCUE, etc conflicts with the emulation and limits the ability to use macro keys. We recommend using the KVM’s USB2.0 port as a workaround if this software if needed. USB2.0 port does not have emulation programming and KVM will not recognize hotkey commands. Please use a powered USB hub to expand USB connectivity.
features: Share 4 computers with 1 monitor, keyboard, mouse. Switch between PC’s with push button, keyboard hotkeys, mouse gestures, or remote control. Can also use as HDMI switch for gaming consoles and streaming devices.
HDMI 2.0 supports UHD 4K60hz (3840x2160), QHD 144hz (2560x1440), 1080P 144hz (1920x1080). Plus Ultrawide 60hz (21:9 & 32:9). HDCP 2.2, HDR10, Dolby Vision. EDID Emulation.
Plug & Play. No software or drivers needed. Supported by IT departments for work-from-home professionals. Separate your work from your personal life without the hassle & clutter!
Transmit audio to monitor via HDMI and/or external speakers via stereo output. Share USB device such as webcam and headset with dedicated USB2.0 port. Great for Zoom and Twitch users.
Supports wired, wireless, mechanical, multimedia, and gaming keyboards. No delay, zero latency keyboard switching and pass-through mode for multimedia keys. READ FAQ & PICS.
U.S. Based TECHNICAL SUPPORT from BuyTESmart available to help resolve all issues.

reviews.title: Shine through is very disappointing
reviews.text: I got these primarily because I liked the orange and black color scheme and the advertised shine through features but the backlit brightness is only fair to poor on these (I'm being generous giving it 3 stars, I think).  Even the cheap $6.00 keycap set ($0.38 after discounts) with a similar color scheme I got from aliexpress is brighter than these.  However, since I need those 1u-sized right Alt, FN and Ctrl keycaps for my Royal Kludge RK96 90% keyboard, I'm not returning therm, though I basically paid $20 for those 3 keycaps :/

features: THE MOST TACTILE SWITCHES IN THE WORLD: With a name like Holy Panda, these switches had to be epic. Made with stems from Halo switches, housing found on Invyr Panda switches, Holy Panda switches combine the best of both worlds.
THE BEST OF BOTH WORLDS: The result is a snappy, tactile switch that is satisfying to type on.

COMPATIBILITY: Holy Panda switches are compatible with plate-mounted Cherry-style PCB's and are perfect for hot-swap compatible barebones kits such as the Drop ALT, CTRL, SHIFT, ENTR, Carina, Planck, and Preonic.
SPECS: Halo Switch Stems, Polycarbonate top housing, Nylon bottom housing, 67g spring, Plate Mount, and MX-compatible.

reviews.title: Excellent tactile
reviews.text:
I have purchased these, lubed Glorious Panda, and Tecsee Purple panda.

This is the best with the other 2 being very close to as good as this. The tactile-ness is a little better than the other both. With the other 2, the tactile is right at the beginning, while this one starts at the beginning and seems to feel the same until bottoming out. All have the nice bump back.

I didn't lube these and they sound and feel great. I think this is my favorite tactile now. But if you're on budget, I would recommend one of the others.

reviews.title: Not a bad one in the bunch
reviews.text: Got the 90 pack and not a broken or bent pin in the entire bunch. Professionally packed and working 100 percent. All switches tested and working fine. A little expensive but worth it for the fast Amazon delivery.

reviews.title: Really cheap look and feel
reviews.text:
These are really cheap. I know you might be thinking, you get what you pay for and these are cheaply priced. However, at the end of the day, keycaps are just plastic and the good ones are just waaay over priced.

First thing first, the actual color differs from the picture. The picture has a nice looking matcha green, the actual product is a little darker green and no where as vibrant. Also, some of the legends on the keycaps are kind of blurry. I am noticing the blurriness on all the non-alpha caps. You can tell these are really cheap and they look pretty bad.

reviews.title: Spacebars do not fit well
reviews.text:
[[VIDEOID:14b0c07be1bf5b90e20f388d925ce8c8]] The keycap set is great in terms of design, modifier key size options, and key legend for quality for price. Fitting for all keys is great, excepts for the large spacebars.
The space bars (both 6.25u and 7u) in my set don’t work smoothly and somehow get the stabs stuck (two different kinds of stabs as well). I tried them on two keyboards (one with 7u and other with 6.25u) and only these spacebars showed problems and the other spacebars I have do not show this problem. I am not sure really whats causing the space bar issue, maybe those pbt space keycaps are slightly bent (as I have read about) but visually they look okay.

reviews.title: Good Quality for the $ - Fits Cherry MX Switches
reviews.text:
This fit my Ducky mechanical keyboard without issue. They seem to be well made and fit tightly in my cherry MX switches. A couple observations, not sure it’s a positive or a negative but a point of observation:

1) They do not come with media key replacements. The blue ones you see in my image are from my original key caps

2) The height of the keys are slightly shorter than my Ducky OEMs. While initially this was a possible concern for me, having typed with the new keys for a couple weeks, seems to not impact typing at all.

3) The blue caps that came with the replacements are not the ones you see in my photo (Enter key). They have a slightly yellow / blue hue to them vs. the Ducky’s I’m using. Wanted my enter key to match the rest of the blue media keys so I mixed and matched. Some may like the blue that comes with the set, I just preferred my original blue keys color.

For the price, they fit the bill, can’t complain. We’ll see long term how the printing of the keys holds up / how quickly they wear down.

Wish list:

- Caps lock key had the word ‘Caps’ on it
- Windows button keys had something besides “Super”.

Overall good purchase.

title: Ganss ALT 71 Wireless/Wired 2.4G + USB Mechanical Keyboard with Cherry MX Clear Switch for Mac and Windows, PBT Keycaps for Desktop and Laptop Computer, 71 Keys (Can Be Customize to 68 Keys Layout)
features: Cherry MX Clear Switches - Medium Tactile and Light Clicky. They are ideal for people who want to use their keyboard for both gaming and typing.

Compact and Portable - Enjoy the freedom of wirelessness with a 2.4G connection. Reliable and fast connects with devices. It saves desk space and fits right in the backpack to carry around. Perfect for home, work and on the go.
Full Keys Anti-ghosting - All 71 keys are conflict free N-Key rollover with no input error for ultimate Gaming performance, it’s more friendly to users, ensuring smooth gaming experience.
Wireless keyboard - Simply connect your portable keyboard with one USB receiver without any software.

PBT Dye Sublimation - With high-quality PBT keycaps and an anti-grease coating, these 71-key keycaps provide top-notch durability and a great feel for your typing. Advanced five-sided sublimation technology makes the pattern not fade.

reviews.title: Only the 3 switches above the arrow keys are hotswapable
reviews.text:
Ganss ALT 71 Wireless/Wired 2.4G + USB Mechanical Keyboard with Cherry MX Brown Switch for Mac and Windows, PBT Keycaps for Desktop and Laptop Computer, 71 Keys (Can Be Customize to 68 Keys Layout)

This Keyboard is weird. I don't know why they made only the 3 switches above the arrow key hotswapable and not the others. I like the customization of the layout, but c'mon. The keyboard sounds terrible and with the limited tuning I was able to do, it sounds and feels a bit better. The wireless works fine and doesn't lose connection. The included keycaps are not great either. I replaced those straight away. If you are looking for a keyboard to mod this is not it.

reviews.title: Compact and functional at a premium price
reviews.text:
The Ganss ALT 71 keyboard is good, but not good enough to justify the price tag. I do really like that it's wireless--the dongle works well and I didn't experience any lag while typing for work or gaming. The keyboard even came with batteries, which was great! The wired option works well, too.

I didn't love the keycaps and replaced them right away with some that I already had, which are more smooth and have a broader key surface. That's a personal preference thing, though.

I've never used clear switches and was excited to give them a try. I usually use red switches, and found the clear switches to be very comfortable. They offer more resistance and shorter travel than red switches. Once I switched to lower profile key caps, I found the noise level to be slightly quieter as well. There is a bit of spring squeak when typing, though. There are also no feet on the keyboard to adjust the height/angle of your typing surface.

Only three of the switches can be removed, though I cannot figure out why you would... It removes functionality (why would you want a page up key, but not page down?). The only reason to do this that I could think of was to more distinctly isolate the arrow keys for gaming.

The keyboard came with a keycap puller and I was able to replace all of the caps without too much trouble. The keyboard is nice and compact and I do like that it's wireless. But for the price, it's very strange that there's no backlighting or truly hot-swappable switches.

reviews.title: Cherry Switches, dual USB/wireless, but missing other features
reviews.text:
I read the specifications several times for the Ganss keyboard, thinking I had missed something. Some things, in fact. The basic product -- the experience of typing on it -- is solid, and it's good to have both wireless and wired alternatives in the same keyboard.

But . . .

At this price point, you are going to do without several features that you might want. See CON.

But what you do get is a compact (no numeric pad, no function key row) keyboard whose touch I would judge to be halfway between gamer ("clacky") and laptop membrane-like (near-silent, minimal key feedback, short throw). The keys are durable, rollover-tolerant, likely top of the line at the key level.

PRO
- Compact
- Durable, with keytop replacement tool supplied
- Well-considered "middle feel" tactile design
- Status lights are below the keyboard, which makes them more visible when accidentally set (e.g., caps lock)
- Molded plastic cover will probably last awhile

CON
- Better keep the supplied manual (an annotated 2-pp diagram), as you may not be able to figure out key combinations after not using this for awhile. Function key access, for instance, is anything but intuitive
- Limited programmability
- No USB hub/auxiliary port (for mouse)
- Not backlit (requires different model from this maker)
- Tilt feature I usually use didn't work well for me YMMV
- I miss having an alternate ENTER key on the right edge somewhere
- The DEL key is oddly placed

Despite these limitations, you might like this model for the feel.

reviews.title: There are Much Better Options for the Price
reviews.text: This board was okay but at $100 it's hard to justify it's price. The build quality is similar to your run of the mill gk61 or sk61, so it's a plastic tray mount case with an some kind of metal plate not sure if it's steel or aluminum. The board isn't hotswap aside from 3 keys in the nav cluster on the right side of the board that can be covered with a plastic plate. I personally think Ganss was really brave to offer Cherry MX Clears as an option as those bottom out at nearly 100g but I'm here for it. The acoustics of the board are pretty bad this thing is loud and I would not recommend it for an office environment unless you really hate your coworkers. The stabilizers were sufficiently lubed and fit well in the plate so they didn't rattle. The keycaps seem like a cheap dye sub set and weren't terrible. The board lacks any kind of backlighting which I find strange as it has north facing switches which is usually the reason to have them that way otherwise they can interference with row 3 of cherry profile keycaps. The board has Bluetooth but doesn't have a rechargeable battery included, just 2 AAA Duracell batteries which I will give them credit for as Duracell is a good brand. Overall the board doesn't really do anything to justify it's steep price point there are much better boards in it's price range that include better switches, backlighting, and way better acoustics.

reviews.title: Compact, Well-Made
reviews.text:
This super-compact mechanical keyboard has a solid feel for a plastic keyboard at this price point. The travel and build quality of the keys feel great, and as expected for MX Clears (which are softer than other Cherry MX switches I have used). Having a detachable USB-C cable plus wireless is the best of all worlds for connectivity and long-term usability, and should be the standard for all keyboards moving forward.

I prefer a larger footprint for my daily driver, but this is a great keyboard if you need portability or work in a small space, and still want the benefits of Cherry MX switches.

n.b. The keyboard is not backlit, which increased battery life, but definitely reduces its "gamer chic" vibe.

reviews.title: I liked it very much until it started disconnecting
reviews.text:
I've only had it for a week and the type C end moves and it disconnects from my keyboard. it's not a lot but enough to power off my key board.

But, aside from that little piece of failure, i really like the sturdiness and the quality of the work on it. It's also at a great price. That's why i'm not returning this item, it's actually really good and as long as the cable is held in place it works great. Maybe it's my WFH wonky setup. I would buy more if i needed more cables, but I'm set for life with cables.

really nice cable, i would recommend to others.

reviews.title: Works great
reviews.text: Purchased for my son.  He says it works great.

reviews.title: Great upgrade for any Keyboard, best with custom rugged builds.
reviews.text: I wanted to get a substitute USB-C cable for my keyboard and found this listing. Was intrigued by the interchangeable inputs as well as the mechanical design. The price seemed steep, but the uses, ergonomics and possible durability of the cable seemed like a good investment. Mine came functional with an included "Kraken Keybaord" sticker, thank you! The only problem I found was that the USB-C male input was a tad long for my ANNE PRO Clone, a Dierya x Kemove DK61 60% Keyboard. It might be my keyboard's own build quality, or that maybe these cables are meant for more rugged custom boards. If I move my keyboard drastically it might disconnect, but luckily that rarely happens. I'm more than satisfied with the cable, more like enamored. I'd buy again for a future custom keyboard build for sure.

title: Hexgears Impulse - Hot-swap Mechanical Keyboard (Box Brown Switches, Pudding Keycaps)
description:
The Hexgears Impulse is available in many amazing configurations.
— Durable PBT Keycaps
PBT is an engineering plastic that resists shine and finger grease. This is far better than the cheap plastic used by major brands. All Impulses use PBT in their doubleshot injection molded keycaps — the main letters won’t ever wear off.
— Awesome RGB Backlighting and RGB Ring Lighting
The Impulse lighting system is on-board, ensuring compatibility with various operating systems. Translucent pudding keycaps showcase its smooth RGB lighting in a way that other keyboards can only dream of. RGB customization options allow you to select an experience that matches your battlestation.
— Survives Your Worst Impulses
Do you have a coffee habit? A cat that spills things on the regular? The Impulse handles liquids like a champ. IP56 water resistance means that the keyboard functions while blasted with water jets. Its curved metal frame is also quite rigid.
— Kailh Box Switches
Kailh Box switches are rated for 80 million presses — way above industry standard. They have drainage ports and self-cleaning housings. Box switches also feel excellent, as they’re less wobbly and noticeably smoother than standard models. Tactile Box switches result in relatively quiet mechanical keyboards, but the clicky versions go all out with a new and improved mechanism.
— Other Features
The Impulse is designed as a plug and play device. Its feature set offers exactly what you need to get up and running fast. 2-stage angle adjustment feet and basic onboard functions like Windows key locking enhance your experience.
Impulse keyboards have a 1-year limited warranty.
features: RGB, Per-key customizable lighting modes, RGB Light Ring
Two-shot extra durable PBT keycaps (Pudding & 2-tone)
Curved metal mounting plate with 2 stage adjustable height feet
IP56 water resistant design, N-key Rollover (NKRO), integrated USB-A cable
Available in Kailh BOX Brown (Tactile), BOX White (Clicky), and Hako Clear (Heavy Tactile) Mechanical Switches
Updated Impulse V2 design with Hot-swap switch sockets (MX-compatible, LED compatible, plate mount)

reviews.title: Good Keyboard, clicky, and positive return
reviews.text:
Hi, I am a bit of a keyboard junky. I have bought probably 10 mechanical keyboards over the last 3 years. I still have an original IBM PC keyboard - that is really stiff and loud. But about this keyboard, I read an online review and said, why not - and I have been using it for several days. First, it sets up easy. Just plug in to get started. Configuring all the lights is more work and not completely intuitive - especially if you lose the little manual 3x4" or so. Personally, I don't care that much for the lighting features on any keyboard - I want to know if I can type quickly without any errors from the keyboard as opposed from me. In general, this keyboard is one of the best. The key depression effort is light to medium - if you rest your fingers on the keys, they will not trigger -which is good. The space bar works well - and I usually find this a problem because I hit it a bit off center - but this one works well. The noise is medium. The clacky sound is the sound of me pressing the keys to the maximum and not the click of the membrane.

I generally like this keyboard and I have tried many from $79 mechanical keys to $200 keyboards. I think this is up there with the best - maybe not all the bells and whistles like volume control, but I never use that. Buy this. Works well. Feels good under the fingers. Type fast!

reviews.title: Recommended for Gaming
reviews.text: Nice keyboard. Has NKRO, macro support, and lots of lighting patterns for the keyboard and ring lighting. Looks very nice in the Pudding keycaps version with full RGB. I ordered it with the Kailh Box Brown switches, which work smoothly, and have a somewhat subtle tactile feedback. I tested it fully, and there was no key chatter detected. Well worth the reasonable asking price and one of the very few current options offering these switches in a commercial build.

reviews.title: Great for typing
reviews.text:
I have the version with white keycaps and "box white" clicky switches. I don't play computer games, so I can't comment on that aspect of the keyboard. But I can say that this is a very good typist's keyboard. The key switches sound like Cherry Blues, but I think these feel smoother, and have less lateral wiggle room.

The keyboard comes with a tiny "user guide" that is no more than a list of keystrokes that control the lighting and macros. I don't care for lighting on a keyboard, so the first thing I tried to do was dim the lights. The "user guide" wasn't very clear on that, so here's how:

While holding down the "Fn" key...
... press the Down arrow to dim the white lights.
... press the "2" key on the numeric keypad to dim the colored perimeter lights.

Keep pressing those keys to dim the lights further. I've now got them completely dimmed, and I hope they stay that way.

One thing to know about this keyboard is that the keycaps aren't recessed. On most keyboards, you can't see or grasp the bottoms of the keycaps, because they fit into cavities formed by the molded plastic deck. This keyboard's top deck is flat metal, without cavities. So there's about a quarter-inch gap between the keycap bottoms and the top of the deck. As a result, it's easy to pull the keycaps. You can even remove the Escape key with your bare fingers. I don't think this is a bad thing; it's just worth knowing about.

Speaking of keycaps, these use a stylized font that sacrifices legibility for fashion. The designer apparently dislikes closed shapes, and created little vertical gaps in the Q, O, zero, 8, etc. Not even the ampersand, pound sign, or the little orbs of the percent sign are spared this annoying treatment. It looks like the dye is flaking away. As a sometimes hunt-and-peck typist, I find this very distracting, as it keeps me from instantly recognizing many of the keys.

I would rate this keyboard with five stars, if not for the inadequate "user guide" and the poorly thought-out keycap font.

reviews.title: Very well built
reviews.text: Solid keys, good feel.  I don't need all of the LEDS, and had to play around with the controls to get just the minimum lighting.  Much better than my old "mushy" wireless keyboard.

Should you not care about that, here is some more info. They look okay in the light, but in the dark there is a strong screen glare off of them that can be distracting.

Not a keyboard, just key caps to "bling out" your existing keyboard.

I purchased the following keyboard and these key caps fit perfectly. The keyboard (not this item, which is only key caps) comes with a red plastic tool that you can use to pop out the key caps:

Redragon K551 Mechanical Gaming Keyboard RGB LED Rainbow Backlit Wired Keyboard with Red Switches for Windows Gaming PC (104 Keys, Black)

I would give it all five stars if it had arrived containing all the key caps advertised in the product listing. It was a surprise prank gift for a coworker, which is why I made do with what we got.

reviews.title: Perfect starter Artisan keycap!
reviews.text:
Edit: purchased the black AND the white one. 10/10

I purchased this keycap as I wanted an artisan but did not want to have to pay resale on ebay or Mechmarket. After a quick search, I was glad to find this combo as it covers the escape key AND the tab key in a matching set. I tried both keycaps on multiple boards and I am thoroughly impressed.

I can easily say that this keycap set will fit both regular MX stems and box stems, as they fit on my box inks and NK creams without issue. I swapped them on 6 different boards just to see how they looked with different sets and did not find there to be any loosening.

Being able to change the "screen" is a fun touch but I am leaving it on the apple logo for nostalgia. The keyboard on the tab key is magnetic and I have found myself playing with it while working today.

Overall this is an easy 10/10 for both price and quality. I highly recommend it for both enthusiasts who want a fun budget set but also for anyone with a keyboard in general!

title: Coral Sea Keycaps 108 PBT Heat Sublimation OEM Profile Keycap for Cherry Mx Gateron Kailh Switch Mechanical Keyboard
features: 【PBT Dye Sublimation】With high-quality PBT keycaps and an anti-grease coating, these 108-key keycaps provide top-notch durability and a great feel for your typing. Advanced five-sided sublimation technology makes the pattern not fade.
【Ukiyoe Style】Exquisite Ukiyo-e style bring a new experience. the keycaps are crafted with extra-thick walls fit for intensive gaming that produce satisfying, solid keystrokes.

【Excellent Craftsmanship】Using injection molding technology, waterproof and dustproof design, easy to clean up dirt. Floating keycap design, uniform light transmission, making the lighting effect obvious.
【OEM Profile Keycaps】 This keycap set is OEM profile which is higher than Cherry profile. It is not easy to fatigue after long-term use and has better comfort.
【Warm Tips】Keycaps only, The keyboard in the picture is only for the demo and is not for sale.

reviews.title: Looks great, types great
reviews.text:
The caps are thick and wide so typing on them is a pleasure. I also love the oriental graphics of ocean waves and whales. The only gripe would be the packaging box is a bit robust in need of better designs and material. Otherwise, this is a great product. Oh also better font selection would be better too, especially the non-capitalized letters on the pause vs page up vs page down vs delete vs insert buttons. They seem inconsistent.

I would pay an extra $5 if this keycap set supports 65% or 68% design.

reviews.title: You get what you pay for
reviews.text:
Overall it's a decent set and a good value. However, I noticed a number of stains on the keys, incorrect legends (the n in the End key), reversed prints (colon/semicolon and apostrophe/quotation were swapped), and misaligned legends (the F9 is not in line with the other F keys).

I would probably not buy from this seller or manufacturer again due to this.

reviews.title: Not great
reviews.text: My son got it for gaming and we returned it after one day because the keys were sticking. That’s clearly not ideal for a gamer.

title: Glorious Aura V2 (Black) - PBT Pudding Keycaps for Mechanical Keyboards - ANSI (US), ISO Compatible - Supports Full Size, TKL, 75%, 60% Layouts
features: ✅Glorious Aura Keycaps V2 : This redesigned 145 key pudding keycap layout is compatible with GMMK Regular/PRO and most keyboard sets
✅Comes in black or white colors
✅Standard OEM Profile: Glorious Aura Pudding Keycap Set features a standard OEM profile that is widely used over many mechanical keyboards
✅Doubleshot injection legends, backlight RGB led compatible (transparent legends and sides), comes with 1 extra Glorious ASCEND ESC key and original Aura ESC key
✅Thick, High-Quality PBT Plastic Keys : Glorious Aura Pudding Keycaps are made with quality material that is resistant to wear, friction, and solvents
✅Transparent Legends, With Transparent Sides : Glorious Aura Pudding Keycaps are designed to let more light through, making your keyboard's RGB lighting more vibrant

✅Updated font compared to Glorious Aura V1 and support for more mechanical keyboards, compatible with layouts with STANDARD BOTTOM ROW. Fully compatible with Glorious GMMK Mechanical Gaming Keyboards (KEYBOARD NOT INCLUDED), please check your keyboard layout for compatibility

reviews.title: Best keycaps I have ever used.
reviews.text:
Bought these for my GMMK pro.

I have owned several mechanical keyboards, but never bought a high-end set of keycaps. Unlike every other set I've ever used, these just seem to ignore dirt and grease. I can stuff my face with a big Mac and go straight to typing, and the caps stay completely clean. I don't know how that works but I won't question it.

Other keyboards require weekly wipe downs, I have never had to do it with these caps.

They're also noticeably built well. These are going to outlast my fingers by a hundred years, likely.

reviews.title: Legend doesn’t glow like other pics
reviews.text: For my Tofu65, I do like these pudding key caps, but I’m not sure why the legends on my key caps aren’t transparent. Everyone who posted reviews with pics show their legends glowing, so I’m not sure why mine are different. My other issue is I got several duplicative keys, and I’m missing a few. For example, I only have one home key and about 4 page down keys. So that’s slightly annoying. I’ve seen some others complain about this similar thing as well. But the feel and look of these key caps are quality. Slightly disappointed.

reviews.title: Great caps work as advertised
reviews.text: Works great

title: VELOCIFIRE Keycap Puller Tool, 3 in 1 Keycap Puller, Stainless Steel Keycap Removal Tool, Key Pulling, Loosen, Tighten Keycap Stem, Expert for Mechanical Keyboard(Black)
features: This wire style keycap puller is a necessary tool for any mechanical keyboard owner. Easily remove and replace keycaps with this keycap removal tool.
Creative two-stage combination design, multifunctional keycap puller, one part is keycap puller, one part is keycap stem adjust.
You just need to place the keycap puller metal at the bottom of the keycap and pull the keycap out vertically.
Round body shape designed for maximum comfort.Compatible for most mechanical keyboards including Cherry MX，Kailh, Outemu, and Content Switches.
Wires can bend outward to fit onto every key except the spacebar. (The spacebar key can be removed by hand after the surrounding keys are removed).

reviews.title: It's good, but it doesn't fit Daskeyboard keys
reviews.text: I have a das keyboard.  This doesn't fit over the keys.  I had to twist and bend it to make it work.  It's not the right tool for a das keyboard.  With that said, it's a good quality tool.  I would buy it again for something with smaller keys.  Just don't bother for the mechanical das keyboard.
